title: Sentinel: Consumer Outbound Pipeline (full flow from local client to producer, VP attachment, retry with new jti)
description: Implement the complete consumer-side outbound request pipeline. When a local client application calls the consumer sentinel, it must: resolve the target ServiceDescriptor from Discovery (or local cache), verify the producer's descriptor signature, select the best producer endpoint (weighted round-robin with health filtering), select matching Verifiable Credentials, build a VP with producer DID as audience, build and sign the ProofClaims envelope, send the request to the producer sentinel, and handle retries by generating a fresh jti+VP per attempt. All telemetry (trace_id, correlation_id) must be propagated.
objectives: Resolve ServiceDescriptor with in-memory cache (TTL: descriptor max_age, max 10 minutes)
Verify producer ServiceDescriptor JWS signature before trusting any endpoint
Select matching VCs (by service_id, env) from local credential store
Build VP with aud=producer_service_did and nonce=new_jti per attempt
Sign ProofClaims envelope and send to selected producer endpoint
Retry logic: up to 3 attempts, exponential backoff (0.5s, 1s, 2s), new jti+VP per attempt
Propagate trace_id and correlation_id through all hops

steps.title: Define the consumer gateway route
steps.detail: In `sentinel/consumer/routes/outbound.py`, register: `router.post('/outbound/{service_id}/{path:path}')` and `router.get('/outbound/{service_id}/{path:path}')` (support all HTTP methods via a catch-all). Handler: `async def proxy_outbound(service_id: str, path: str, request: Request)`. Extract all original headers (excluding hop-by-hop), body bytes, and query parameters. Inject into pipeline. Respond with producer's response status+body+headers (scrub internal headers from producer before returning to local client).

steps.title: ServiceDescriptor resolution with cache
steps.detail: Class `DescriptorCache` (in `consumer/descriptor_cache.py`): in-memory dict keyed by `(service_id, env)`. TTL is `min(descriptor.max_age_seconds, 600)` — never cache longer than 10 minutes regardless of what descriptor says. On cache miss: call `DiscoveryClient.resolve_service(service_id, env)` (TASK-040). On cache hit: verify descriptor signature is still valid (exp not exceeded). Expose `async get(service_id, env) -> ServiceDescriptor`. If Discovery is unreachable and cache is empty, raise `ServiceNotFound` (503). If cache is stale but Discovery unreachable, serve stale with WARNING log.

steps.title: Verify producer ServiceDescriptor signature
steps.detail: Before using any endpoint from the descriptor, verify the JWS signature using `verify_jws(descriptor.signed_jwt, resolver)`. The signer is the producer DID from `descriptor.producer_did`. Verify: (1) JWS signature valid. (2) `producer_did` in descriptor payload matches `iss` in JWS. (3) `exp` not exceeded (descriptors have max 10 min TTL). (4) `service_id` in descriptor matches requested service_id. If verification fails: evict from cache and raise `DescriptorInvalid` (502). This prevents DNS/cache-poisoning attacks from redirecting traffic to rogue endpoints.

steps.title: Endpoint selection with weighted round-robin
steps.detail: Class `EndpointSelector` in `consumer/endpoint_selector.py`. Descriptor contains `endpoints: [{url, weight, health_status}]`. Filter out endpoints with `health_status == 'draining'` or `'unhealthy'`. Apply weighted random selection: assign cumulative weights, draw `random.uniform(0, total_weight)`. If all endpoints are unhealthy/draining: fallback to returning any available (non-draining) endpoint with WARNING log. If no endpoints at all: raise `NoEndpointsAvailable` (503). Track per-endpoint consecutive failure count — after 3 failures, mark as unhealthy locally for 30s (circuit breaker per endpoint).

steps.title: Credential selection for VP
steps.detail: Function `select_credentials(service_id: str, env: str, credential_store: CredentialStore) -> list[str]`. Query local credential store (TASK-038) for credentials where: `credentialSubject.service_id == service_id AND credentialSubject.env == env AND exp > now AND status != revoked`. If no credentials found: raise `NoCredentialAvailable` (403 to local client — not the producer's fault). Select all matching (consumer may hold both identity + access credentials — include both types). If multiple of same type, prefer the one with latest `exp` (most recently issued).

steps.title: Build VP and ProofClaims per attempt
steps.detail: For each attempt (up to max_retries): (1) Generate new `jti = str(uuid4())`. (2) Call `create_vp(vcs=credentials, holder_did=consumer_did, holder_key=consumer_key, aud=descriptor.service_did, nonce=jti, env=env, exp_seconds=60)`. (3) Call `ProofClaimsBuilder.build(method, url, body, aud=descriptor.service_did, env=env)` using same jti from step 1 (jti is shared between proof and VP nonce to create binding). (4) Build headers via `build_secure_headers(proof_jwt, vp_jwt)`. (5) Append original request headers (minus Authorization which is replaced). CRITICAL: steps 1-4 MUST be repeated fresh for each retry — never reuse jti or VP.

steps.title: Send request and handle response
steps.detail: Use shared `httpx.AsyncClient` (from TASK-037 app state, max_connections=100). Call: `response = await client.request(method, endpoint_url + '/' + path, headers=headers, content=body, params=query_params, timeout=httpx.Timeout(connect=5.0, read=30.0, write=10.0))`. On success (2xx): pass response back to local client, preserving status code and content-type. On 4xx from producer: do NOT retry (auth failure is not transient) — return 502 with `{error: 'UPSTREAM_AUTH_FAILURE', upstream_status: 4xx}` to local client. On 5xx or timeout: retry with backoff. On final retry failure: return 503 to local client.

steps.title: Retry policy and backoff
steps.detail: Max 3 total attempts (1 initial + 2 retries). Backoff: attempt 1→2: sleep 0.5s, attempt 2→3: sleep 1.0s. Jitter: add `random.uniform(0, 0.1)` to each sleep to avoid retry storms. On each retry: (1) regenerate jti + VP + proof (step 6 above). (2) Re-select endpoint (may select a different one if previous failed). (3) Log: `level=WARNING event=outbound_retry attempt={n} service_id={id} endpoint={url} reason={reason}`. Do NOT retry on: 400, 401, 403, 422 from producer (these are definitive rejections).

steps.title: Telemetry propagation
steps.detail: On each outbound request: (1) Propagate `traceparent` header (W3C Trace Context) sampled from current OTel span. (2) Set `X-Correlation-ID` to the correlation_id received from local client (or generate if absent). (3) Add `X-Forwarded-For` with local client IP if not already present (but scrub before forwarding to avoid leaking internal topology). (4) Create OTel child span `sentinel.outbound_request` with attributes: `service_id`, `endpoint`, `attempt_number`, `vp_jti` (for correlation). (5) Record `outbound_requests_total{service_id, status}` Prometheus counter.

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: Test file: `tests/integration/consumer/test_outbound_pipeline.py`. Cases: (1) successful request end-to-end with mock producer; (2) descriptor signature invalid — request rejected before send; (3) no credentials available — 403 returned to client; (4) producer returns 401 — no retry, 502 returned; (5) producer returns 503 then 200 — retry succeeds with NEW jti (assert second jti != first jti); (6) all retries fail — 503 returned to client; (7) no endpoints available — 503 immediately; (8) endpoint circuit breaker trips after 3 failures. Use `respx` for HTTPX mocking.

files.sentinel/consumer/descriptor_cache.py: DescriptorCache with TTL and signature verification
files.sentinel/consumer/endpoint_selector.py: EndpointSelector with weighted round-robin and local circuit breaker

error_handling.trigger: Descriptor JWS signature invalid or expired
No matching VC for service_id+env in local store

security_constraints: Producer ServiceDescriptor signature MUST be verified before trusting any endpoint URL — prevents SSRF via descriptor injection
New jti+VP MUST be generated per retry — never reuse jti
Hop-by-hop headers (Connection, Transfer-Encoding, Upgrade) must be stripped before forwarding
Internal endpoint URLs from descriptor must not be returned to local clients in error messages
Timeout must be enforced to prevent producer-side slow-loris attacks holding consumer goroutines
acceptance_criteria: Successful request completes end-to-end with correct VP and proof headers
New jti is generated for each retry attempt (asserted in tests)
Descriptor with invalid signature is rejected before any request is sent
4xx from producer does not trigger retry
503 returned to local client after all retries exhausted
Trace context propagated in all outbound requests
